title: 'Hat-trick of defeat for Congress certain': PM Modi takes 'rajkumar' dig at Rahul Gandhi in Assam
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi launched a strong attack on Congress in Assam, dubbing Rahul Gandhi a "Rajkumar" and predicting electoral defeats. He expressed confidence in the BJP-led NDA securing a "hattrick" in the state, highlighting development under Sarbananda Sonowal and Himanta Biswa Sarma. PM Modi also promised a Uniform Civil Code and positioned Assam as a future tech hub.

title: 10k strikes, 40 top officials killed in 40 secs: Israel outlines scale of Iran offensive
content: Israel's military revealed extensive "Operation Roar of the Lion" details, reporting over 800 sorties and 10,000 strikes against Iranian-linked targets. They claim to have eliminated 40 top Iranian officials in a swift 40-second operation. Simultaneously, Iran's IRGC reported targeting US and Israeli assets in the Persian Gulf and UAE.

title: Mumbai auto driver lifts kid by legs, smashes his head on ground after tiff with father
content: An autorickshaw driver in Palghar brutally assaulted a four-year-old boy, slamming him to the ground and smashing his head against an iron rod after an argument with the child's father. The critically injured boy is battling for life in the hospital, and the accused has been arrested.

title: Oracle layoffs: Company sets this severance condition for laid-off employees
content: Oracle has started its largest-ever layoffs, impacting thousands globally. Employees must sign termination documents via DocuSign to receive severance, which appears less generous than competitors.  These cuts, potentially reaching 30,000, aim to fund a massive AI data center expansion, a move that has placed the company under significant financial strain.

title: 'Bas ho gaya': Rishabh Pant ends Nitish Rana–Digvesh Rathi feud
content: Rishabh Pant played peacemaker between Nitish Rana and Digvesh Rathi, resolving their on-field spat during the Delhi Premier League. The LSG captain's intervention helped the two cricketers patch things up ahead of the IPL 2026 clash between Lucknow Super Giants and Delhi Capitals. Both teams are looking to improve after missing the playoffs in the previous season.

title: If NDA comes to power, it won't hesitate to rename Tamil Nadu as 'Dakshin Pradesh': Stalin
content: Tamil Nadu CM MK Stalin urged voters to defeat the AIADMK-BJP alliance, accusing them of betraying minorities and Sri Lankan Tamils by supporting the CAA. He questioned AIADMK's leadership in the alliance and warned of dire consequences if the NDA comes to power, emphasizing the need to preserve Tamil Nadu's identity.

title: Why is stock market up today? Nifty50 above 22,900; BSE Sensex up over 2,000 points
content: Stock market today: Nifty50 and BSE Sensex rallied strongly on Wednesday on hopes of an end to the US-Iran war. While Nifty50 went above 22,900, BSE Sensex was up 2,000 points.

title: Oracle layoffs: Employees in India, US, & other regions receive email at 6am
content: Oracle has initiated widespread global layoffs, impacting employees in the US, India, and other regions without prior notice.  Termination emails, sent directly from leadership, cited organizational changes as the reason, with affected teams seeing significant reductions.  This move is reportedly to fund Oracle's ambitious, debt-financed AI infrastructure expansion, potentially impacting thousands of jobs.

title: 'Only a partial, staggered increase': Govt issues clarification on jet fuel price hike
content: The government has limited the rise in jet fuel prices for domestic airlines to 25 percent. This decision aims to ease the financial burden on airlines and air travelers. International routes will face the full price increase. Meanwhile, non-scheduled operators are experiencing a more than double surge in jet fuel costs.

title: 'Dhurandhar2' BO Day 14: Ranveer Singh film eyes Rs 1000 crore net milestone
content: 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' has shattered records, officially crossing the Rs. 900 crore India net milestone. In just two weeks, the Aditya Dhar directorial has collected a staggering Rs.903.22 crore domestic net, surpassing the lifetime totals of almost every Indian blockbuster to date. Led by Ranveer Singh's career-defining performance as Hamza, the film has shown incredible resilience against the IPL 2026 matches, maintaining massive footfalls across theatres nationwide. With a current India gross of Rs. 1,081.31 crore and a global haul nearing Rs. 1,400 crore, the spy epic featuring Sanjay Dutt and Arjun Rampal is now chasing the historic Rs. 1,000 crore India net club.

title: FIFA World Cup: Iraq end 40-year wait, Italy crash out again
content: Iraq secured the final FIFA World Cup 2026 spot by defeating Bolivia 2-1 in a playoff, marking their first appearance since 1986 despite significant off-field challenges. Meanwhile, Italy suffered another heartbreaking qualification failure, losing to Bosnia and Herzegovina 4-1 on penalties, meaning they will miss a third consecutive World Cup.

title: $340 bn Anthropic that wiped trillions from stock market has source code of its tool leaked
content: AI firm Anthropic suffered a significant source code leak of its Claude Code agent, exposing unreleased features like an always-on AI and a pet system. This marks the third such incident for the company, which is reportedly preparing for a massive $380 billion IPO. The leak offers a rare glimpse into the company's development practices.

title: 'Doesn't attract the kind of money that ... ': Why Zampa chose PSL over IPL
content: Adam Zampa was named Player of the Match for his stellar 2/11 spell in Karachi Kings' PSL victory. He explained his decision to choose the PSL over the IPL, citing a lack of IPL interest and the perceived value of his skill set relative to the tournament's commitment. Zampa is enjoying his time in the PSL.

title: GST collections rise 8.2% in March 2026 to hit Rs 1.78 lakh crore
content: Domestic GST revenue reached Rs 1.46 lakh crore, registering a growth of 5.9%, while revenue from imports was recorded at Rs 0.54 lakh crore, rising sharply by 17.8% during the period.

title: Rupee at 100? Currency may weaken further against dollar as oil rises, Middle East conflict persists
content: Even prior to the conflict, the rupee was facing downward pressure due to widening external imbalances and persistent capital outflows. The surge in oil prices has intensified these challenges for the world’s third-largest crude importer.

title: Shreyas Iyer, Neeraj Chopra, Hardik Pandya: Why India’s top stars are becoming their own agents
content: India’s Rs 1,350 crore sports celebrity endorsement market is undergoing a dramatic transformation as elite athletes like Shreyas Iyer, Neeraj Chopra, Hardik Pandya, and Ishan Kishan break away from traditional agencies to assert control over their own brands and careers. By seeing themselves as businesses, these athletes are forming in-house offices and management firms, resulting in endorsement deals that are often up to three times more lucrative than before.

title: Why chef Sanjeev Kapoor was denied permission to wear chef coat for Padma Shri ceremony
content: Celebrity chef Sanjeev Kapoor was initially denied wearing his chef coat for the Padma Shri Award ceremony in 2017, being asked to wear traditional Indian attire instead. He insisted on wearing his uniform, which he considers a symbol of pride for the chef community. Ultimately, he received the award in his chef coat, setting a precedent.

title: Israel targets Iran factory, alleges fentanyl use in chemical weapons; Tehran denies
content: Israel claims it struck an Iranian pharmaceutical facility, alleging it supplied fentanyl for a chemical weapons program. Iran confirmed the strike on the Tofigh Daru factory, asserting it produces only hospital drugs. Both nations reported the incident occurred on Tuesday.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi reveals what fueled his 15-ball fifty vs CSK
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi continued his sensational IPL form, smashing a 15-ball half-century against Chennai Super Kings. This remarkable feat, achieved after eating a sweet, set up Rajasthan Royals' dominant win. Former India spinner Ravichandran Ashwin lauded the young talent but urged patience for his senior India debut.

title: Will UAE enter Iran war? Abu Dhabi pushes US, allies to reopen Strait of Hormuz by force
content: The UAE is reportedly considering military intervention against Iran, a significant shift from its previous neutral stance.  Repeated Iranian attacks and the disruption of the vital Strait of Hormuz have pushed Abu Dhabi to explore options, including supporting US-led efforts to reopen the oil route. This move carries substantial risks of further retaliation.

title: Assam's re-contesting MLAs get 80% richer in 5 yrs; which party saw biggest jump?
content: Assam's re-contesting MLAs have seen a significant surge in their declared wealth, with average assets jumping 80% from Rs 4.17 crore in 2021 to Rs 7.52 crore by 2026. This substantial increase highlights a notable shift in the financial standing of legislators across various parties as they seek re-election.

title: Rs 80L package, US house: More skeletons tumble out in B'luru techie couple's suicide
content: A techie couple died by suicide in Bengaluru within minutes of each other, reportedly due to job loss and mounting anxiety. The husband was found dead in a locked bedroom, and his wife jumped from the 18th floor shortly after. The couple had moved to Bengaluru to keep their interfaith marriage private.

title: IPL 2026: Why has BCCI punished Punjab Kings skipper Shreyas Iyer?
content: Punjab Kings skipper Shreyas Iyer faced a Rs 12 lakh fine for a slow over-rate in their IPL 2026 opener against Gujarat Titans. Despite the disciplinary action, the team secured a thrilling three-wicket victory, thanks to a stellar 72-run debut from Cooper Connolly, who steered them home in a tense chase.

title: This 96-year-old grandmother went back to school, scored 98%
content: A 96-year-old Kerala woman, Karthyayani Amma, defied age and illiteracy by enrolling in a literacy program. She astonishingly scored 98% in her first-ever exam, excelling in reading, writing, and math. Her remarkable achievement, born from a personal desire to learn, inspired a nation and earned her prestigious awards, proving education knows no age limit.

title: IPL mid-game drama: Tewatia forced to change bat during PBKS vs GT
content: An unusual incident saw Rahul Tewatia's bat fail an official gauge test during an IPL 2026 match, forcing a change before he could bat. Despite this brief pause, Gujarat Titans posted 162, but Punjab Kings ultimately chased down the target. Captain Shubman Gill felt their batting was slightly below par, though he praised Prasidh Krishna's bowling effort.

title: Relief for scheduled airlines: Jet fuel prices up by 8.5%; flyers spared steep airfare hikes
content: Jet fuel prices for scheduled Indian carriers have risen by approximately 8.5% in April, a move that offers relief to airlines and averts significant airfare hikes for average travelers. However, prices for non-scheduled and charter flights have more than doubled, impacting the ultra-rich.

title: Oil prices today: Crude climbs as markets weigh Trump’s signal on possible end to Iran war
content: Oil prices surged on Wednesday, with Brent crude surpassing $100 per barrel and WTI also climbing. This rise is attributed to ongoing supply risk concerns, despite potential diplomatic progress. Analysts highlight that even a swift end to the conflict may not immediately ease tight supplies due to infrastructure damage and the critical Strait of Hormuz.

title: 'I don't fear criticism': Arjun Tendulkar's no-nonsense reply goes viral
content: Arjun Tendulkar, son of Sachin Tendulkar, will play for Lucknow Super Giants in IPL 2026 after being picked at his base price.  He revealed his calm demeanor stems from playing with his five dogs in Goa.  Tendulkar previously played two seasons with Mumbai Indians, taking three wickets and scoring 13 runs in five matches.

title: US-Iran war hits Sensex: Rs 51L crore wiped out, $12bn FII outflow; end to selloff?
content: The selloff by foreign institutional investors (FIIs) has been particularly pronounced. Rupee has seen its worst financial year in over 14 years, breaching the 95 per dollar mark in trade on the last trading day (March 30) of the fiscal year.

title: 'Takes Muslim votes, builds temples': Expelled TMC leader Humayun Kabir targets Mamata
content: Aam Janata Unnayan Party chief Humayun Kabir accused West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee of neglecting Muslim voters while favouring Hindus with temple construction. Kabir, now allied with AIMIM's Asaduddin Owaisi, vowed to unseat Banerjee, promising better financial aid to women and emphasizing the need for independent Muslim political leadership for development.

title: Is Hanuman Jayanti on April 1 or 2? Know correct date, shubh muhurat and significance
content: Hanuman Jayanti, celebrating Lord Hanuman's birth, is observed on April 2, 2026. This auspicious festival honors the deity's devotion, strength, and courage. Devotees engage in prayers, offer special items like sindoor and jasmine oil, and recite Hanuman Chalisa and Sundarkand to seek blessings. Temples are adorned for the grand celebration.

title: Your body may warn you weeks before a heart attack, are you missing the signs?
content: Heart disease remains a leading cause of death, with many heart attacks preceded by subtle, often overlooked symptoms like unusual fatigue or mild chest discomfort. These early warning signs can appear days or weeks before an event, and may differ between men and women. Ignoring these signals can have severe consequences.

title: Shreyas Iyer's sister's bhangra sets internet on fire after Punjab Kings win - watch
content: Punjab Kings secured a thrilling three-wicket victory over Gujarat Titans, thanks to an unbeaten 72 from Cooper Connolly.  While Connolly's stellar performance clinched the win, captain Shreyas Iyer's sister, Shresta, stole the spotlight with a viral bhangra celebration post-match. Iyer also provided an update on his wrist injury.

title: Bihar woman dragged on road, groped, clothes torn; act filmed as onlookers watch, 2 held
content: In Nalanda, Bihar, a disturbing incident unfolded when a married woman was subjected to public groping and an attempted gang rape, captured in a video that quickly spread online. Following her brave report to authorities, two of the three alleged assailants have been taken into custody.

title: Price of commercial LPG cylinders hiked by Rs 195.50 in Delhi
content: The increase in price comes despite the Delhi government increasing the allocation of commercial LPG cylinders to 50% of the average daily consumption, raising supplies from 1,800 to 4,500 cylinders (19 kg equivalent) per day in line with directives from the Centre. In Delhi, the price has increased by Rs 195.50, while in Kolkata, the hike stands at Rs 218.

title: Parliament Budget Session LIVE: Kharge, Nadda face off over BJP MP's remark; Lok Sabha discusses A.P. Reorganisation (Amendment) Bill
content: Lok Sabha proceedings resumed after a brief adjournment due to the ruckus over the FCRA Amendment Bill by the Opposition.

title: ‘BJP loot diwas’: Congress slams price hike of essential commodities
content: Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge said while the common citizens are hoping for relief, the BJP leadership remains entirely focused on "looting the public and plundering the nation amidst this crisis"

title: JNU failing on social justice, SC/ST enrolment down 25%: Digvijaya Singh in Rajya Sabha
content: Mr. Singh also flagged 89 pending faculty promotion cases at JNU, of which 62 had exceeded the stipulated processing period, saying the delays were affecting career progression and PhD supervision capacity.

title: LPG and fuel crisis LIVE: Jet fuel prices more than doubled to ₹2.07 lakh/kl; commercial LPG up ₹195.50
content: This is the first time ever that aviation turbine fuel prices have crossed the ₹2 lakh per kilolitre mark; domestic cooking gas LPG rates, which were last hiked by ₹60 per 14.2 kg cylinder on March 7, remain unchanged

title: West Bengal Assembly election: Candidates scrub utensils, fry fritters to seek votes
content: One candidate washed utensils in a village home, while another carried buckets of water for an elderly woman, and the third shaved a voter at a roadside saloon

title: Commercial LPG prices rise 10%; government caps ATF hike on domestic flights at 25%
content: The government stated that OMCs’ under-recovery from cylinders presently stand at ₹380 for every cylinder

title: U.S. report flags high import duties, non-tariff barriers in India
content: The report also alleged that India's tariff rates are announced with the annual budget and are modified on an ad hoc basis through notifications in the Gazette without the opportunity for public comment

title: SIR in Bengal: 47 lakh objections disposed of till March 31, Calcutta HC Chief Justice informs Supreme Court
content: A Bench led by Chief Justice Surya Kant said they were apprised that by April 7, all objections will be decided

title: Free IPL tickets for Karnataka legislators likely to further narrow down tickets for fans
content: Interestingly, under existing ticketing norms, when a fan purchases tickets through official channels, a restriction limits each user to two tickets. In contrast, MLAs will now receive three “free tickets.”

title: Assembly Election LIVE: PM Modi to address two poll rallies in Assam
content: The BJP released its manifesto for Assam, promising to work towards implementing UCC, protecting the land, heritage, and dignity of indigenous people, and pushing its development agenda

title: Lapses in duty by Census officials can invite three years in prison
content: A letter from the Registrar General and Census Commissioner of India to Chief Secretaries of States highlights penalties under Section 11 of the Census Act, 1948, making compliance with Census duties legally enforceable

title: Census 2027 to be entirely in digital mode starting today
content: The exercise, originally scheduled for 2021, has been delayed due to COVID-19 and is now being taken up in 2027

title: Government releases 33 FAQs for first phase of Census
content: The portal has a list of Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) to make it easy for the public to answer questions posed during the 16th Census of the country

title: PM Modi visits tea garden in poll-bound Assam, interacts with women workers
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi is on a day-long visit to the State to address two campaign rallies for BJP candidates

title: T.N. Assembly election: BJP may try to split DMK alliance even if it gets a majority: Thol. Thirumavalavan
content: ‘I’ve wanted to contest in the Assembly election since 2021. How we function in the Assembly shapes the public perception. Going to Parliament is an honour. But, when I look at Tamil Nadu politics, the BJP’s strategy of gradually weakening regional parties and establishing itself is a cause for concern’

title: Census 2027: How to do self-enumeration - a step-by-step guide
content: Census 2027 will be completely digital, with enumerators recording data from house visits on mobile applications.

title: Looking into the continuing costs of India’s COVID-19 policy
content: Six years since the COVID-19 pandemic, the question that three books collectively pose is not whether India’s response succeeded or failed; the evidence on that is substantial. It is whether those who bear the costs of policy failure have been taken seriously enough

title: Government proposing to up Lok Sabha size by 50%; move will disadvantage southern states: Congress
content: Congress leader Jairam Ramesh said Telangana Chief Minister Revanth Reddy has already raised an alarm over the issue and others may very well follow as this proposal becomes officially public

title: Telangana Gig Workers Union welcome bill ensuring social security and dignity
content: The Bill provides statutory recognition to platform-based gig workers and proposes the creation of a tripartite welfare board comprising representatives of the government, workers, and aggregators. It also mandates registration of workers with a unique identification system and requires aggregators to register within a stipulated period.

title: Bengaluru court castigates investigation officer of Lokayukta police and assessing officials for ‘serious lapses and misleading reports’ in corruption case
content: The court found that V. Muniyappa, a former Managing Director of the Karnataka Council for Technological Upgradation, had accumulated disproportionate assets worth ₹4.13 crore — a staggering 170% above his legitimate income — during the check period from December 9, 1982, to July 15, 2014.

title: Radikaa Sarathkumar pulls off ₹75-crore ‘Thaai Kizhavi’ power move
content: As her Tamil blockbuster Thaai Kizhavi releases on OTT soon, Radikaa Sarathkumar rewrites rules of profit-sharing and ageing heroines in Tamil industry

title: 40 bags of toor dal worth ₹2.40 lakh stolen from godown in Kalaburagi district of Karnataka
content: In one of the cases, a gang broke open the lock of a godown and stole 40 bags of toor dal worth about ₹2.40 lakh

title: SIT detains son of rape accused ‘godman’ Kharat for questioning, hunt on for wife
content: At least 10 FIRs, eight for alleged sexual assault or exploitation and two for cheating, have been registered against Mr. Kharat at Sarkarwada police station in Nashik, while the SIT has received more than 100 complaints over the phone against him

title: Actor-politicians and their crusade against corruption
content: Actor and TVK founder C. Joseph Vijay’s anti-corruption plank is nothing new as the film stars floating their own parties in Tamil Nadu have taken up this theme to earn a name for themselves and garner a massive vote

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: Independents, the rebels who dare to raise the banner of protest, are reshaping political landscape
content: A wave of Independent candidates is reshaping Kerala’s electoral landscape by challenging their former party affiliations and garnering support from political rivals. While it is the UDF that has either fielded or supported the largest number of dissidents and rebels in this election, the LDF has put its weight behind 10 candidates.

title: Kerala Assembly elections: RJD’s Tejashwi Yadav joins LDF campaign; Pinarayi Vijayan hails support
content: In a post on X, Kerala CM says Tejashwi Yadav’s presence will bolster efforts to ‘protect the country’s secular fabric’

title: John Brittas slams government for treating FCRA data as ‘National Secrecy’; says all his queries were disallowed in RS
content: CPI(M) MP alleges Parliament is being denied basic regulatory information on FCRA licence cancellations and dashboard access ahead of the new amendment bill

title: T.N. Assembly election: Boycott NDA to protect Tamil Nadu’s rights, says Stalin
content: Accusing Edappadi K. Palaniswami of becoming a branch secretary of the BJP, he asserts that the NDA has to be uprooted completely to prevent Tamil Nadu from becoming a land of violence

title: New Income Tax Rules 2026: All you need to know about the new tax regime
content: The Income Tax Act 2025 is designed to replace the 1961 Act, which the Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman described as a “maze” created by over 4,000 amendments

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: UDF seeks successor to K. Babu as LDF, NDA sharpen contest in Thripunithura
content: Sensing an opportunity in the political vacuum created by K. Babu’s retirement, the LDF has fielded its sitting MLA from neighbouring Vypeen, K.N. Unnikrishnan. The UDF has chosen Kochi Deputy Mayor Deepak Joy to sustain its dominance. The NDA has ceded the constituency to its new ally, Twenty20, which has picked actor Anjali Nair.

title: Kerala Assembly polls: Every election has a new script in Aranmula
content: The constituency was formed in 2008 and the UDF won in the 2011 Assembly election. The LDF turned the tables in the next two elections, with Veena George emerging the winner, while the UDF held an edge in the Lok Sabha and local body polls

title: West Asia crisis may push up realty construction costs by 5% in near term
content: Construction schedules are also likely to be affected because of shortage of materials and resources if the conflict prolongs

title: Kerala Assembly elections 2026 | Constituency watch
content: A collection of stories on some of the key constituencies in Kerala that are set to elect their representatives to Kerala Legislative Assembly in 2026

title: How NIMHANS–Namma 112 integration will route mental health distress calls in Bengaluru
content: The integration between TeleMANAS and Namma 112 has designed around three core factors including location-based routing, structured risk screening at the first point of contact, and bi-directional escalation between counselling and police response.

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: Women’s issues weaponised for smear campaigns, allege critics
content: Women’s safety has seldom emerged as a key issue influencing poll agendas, says writer and academic J. Devika

title: Case booked against TVK candidate in Poonamallee for alleged sexual harassment
content: On March 31, 2026, the victim, accompanied by her husband, submitted a complaint at the Tiruvallur District Superintendent of Police's office, seeking action against Prakash

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: AIIMS, high-speed rail network on BJP manifesto
content: NDA releases its ‘Vikasita Keralam Vision’ manifesto that promises a radical job revolution and a “politics of performance” to transform the State’s economic and social landscape

title: Tamil Nadu Election 2026: Full list of AIADMK candidates
content: All India Anna Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam or the AIADMK, the party led by Edappadi K. Palaniswami, is contesting in 167 seats as part of the National Democratic Alliance (NDA) in Tamil Nadu during the upcoming Assembly election in the State.

title: Kerala Assembly polls: 5,836 opt for home voting in Thrissur
content: On Monday (March 30,2026), the first day, 2,907 voters cast their votes, while 2,929 voted on Tuesday.

title: Kalaburagi City Corporation projects ₹5.92 crore deficit in ₹215 crore budget
content: Major revenue sources are ₹37.89 crore from corporation-owned properties, ₹6.73 crore from building licence fees, ₹2.29 crore from trade licences, ₹2.34 crore from solid waste management cess, and ₹12.84 crore from penalties on property tax arrears

title: India’s untapped ocean ingredient: The rise of native seaweed
content: From sargassum to sea grapes, native seaweed varieties are emerging as a sustainable and flavour-rich ingredient in India’s evolving restaurant kitchens

title: In Assam, BJP candidates not among richest top 5: report
content: The number of candidates with declared criminal cases has dipped marginally from 2021, says an analysis by Association for Democratic Reforms and Assam Election Watch

title: West Asia conflict: Congress spreading panic for political gains like 'political vultures', says PM Modi
content: Mr. Modi accused Congress of indulging in rumour-mongering and trying to instigate people to queue up at fuel pumps and spread public disorder

title: Terrorist killed in ongoing Ganderbal operation in Kashmir: Army
content: The Army on Tuesday (March 31) said the joint search operation was launched in a jungle area of Ganderbal after receiving specific intelligence input

title: Tiger stepping away from golf to seek treatment
content: In a statement four days after his arrest in Florida, Tiger Woods said Tuesday he's "committed to taking the time needed to return to a healthier, stronger, and more focused place, both personally and professionally."

title: Portugal outclasses U.S. amid Pulisic goal drought
content: The United States dropped its second match in a row Tuesday, losing to Portugal in a friendly at Mercedes-Benz Stadium in Atlanta to conclude its March international window.

title: Goodell backs Rooney Rule amid litigation threat
content: NFL commissioner Roger Goodell said he believes the Rooney Rule is "consistent" with hiring laws after recently being threatened by Florida's attorney general with potential civil litigation if the rule was not eliminated.

title: Ump Bucknor in spotlight again after missed call
content: Umpire C.B. Bucknor, who had the poorest results among umpires in MLB's new ABS system last weekend, had another call overturned at first base during Tuesday's Brewers-Rays game.

title: Italy miss out on 3rd straight WC in shootout loss
content: Italy has missed out on a third-consecutive World Cup after losing to Bosnia and Herzegovina in a penalty shootout on Tuesday in Zenica.

title: Ovechkin extends record with 20th 30-goal season
content: Alex Ovechkin scored twice and hit the 30-goal mark for the record-extending 20th time in his NHL career, and the Washington Capitals beat the Philadelphia Flyers 6-4 on Tuesday night.

title: Hurley: I thought ref was looking to 'chest-bump'
content: UConn coach Dan Hurley downplayed his bizarre interaction with an official at the end of Sunday's NCAA tournament victory against Duke, saying he thought the referee was looking to "chest-bump me to celebrate."

title: With lockout possible, MLBPA war chest at $415M
content: MLB players have accumulated $415 million in U.S. Treasury securities, cash and other investments as they prepare for the possibility of an extended lockout when the CBA expires on Dec. 1.

title: 📉 2026 Pain Index: Where does Duke's loss rank?
content: Some losses are understandable in the NCAA tournament. Others will haunt us forever (see: Duke). We rank them all.

content: Annual NFL owners meeting wrapped up Tuesday, and 30 of our reporters shared all the buzz from Phoenix.

content: Colts, Chiefs, Browns and Patriots owners among those talking about feasibility of expanding schedule.

content: Roberto Martinez might think it's "worthless" to read into any results ahead of the World Cup, but alarm bells must surely be ringing in the USMNT camp over Christian Pulisic's 14-match goal drought.

content: Lakers? Cavs? Warriors? A wild card? Before LeBron James makes his decision this summer, sources around the league weigh in.

content: We took 20 NFL draft prospects who could get picked in Rounds 1 and 2 and found ideal scheme matches based on what each player does best.

content: Experts on each national team to qualify for their first World Cup help us get to know their country.

content: The pressure of expectation derailed the Huskies' bid for a three-peat. Perspective has them back in contention.

title: 2026 rankings: Profiling the top 35 QBs
content: the 2024 NFL MVP has finished in the top 10 in passing TDs six seasons in a row and remains a major threat with his legs . despite seeing less passing volume the past two seasons (14th or lower in pass attempts both years), he has finished second among quarterbacks in fantasy points . his 21.4 fantasy points per game in 2025 was actually his lowest since 2019 .

title: 2026 World Cup European playoffs recap: Bosnia deny Italy for third straight World Cup
content: Gab Marcotti reacts to Italy failing to qualify for a third straight World Cup after losing to Bosnia and Herzegovina on penalties. (1:37) The final spots for the 2026  FIFA World Cup  will be decided Tuesday. In Europe, four matches determined which teams will be coming to this summer's tournament in North America. Sweden  defeated  Poland  and  Türkiye  defeated  Kosovo  to book their spots in the 90 minutes as the two winning teams returned to the World Cup for the first time since 2018 and 2002, respectively. However, two games went to penalties, and in the end, it was  Czechia  that eliminated  Denmark  to return to the World Cup for the first time since 2006, and finally we had  Bosnia and Herzegovina  defeat  Italy  as the  Azzurri  failed to qualify for the World Cup a third straight time. Enjoy all that happened from Tuesday's matches!
